hero.question: Will Arizona win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 20.9%, the crowd is pricing Arizona as a middle-tier title contender, roughly equivalent to a 1-seed's baseline tournament equity, which undershoots a program with Tommy Lloyd's coaching tier and a recruiting class that's quietly elite for March. The +2.2 point move signals sharps waking up to the gap between Arizona's regular season floor and its tournament ceiling, but the market's still sleeping on how few teams can match their perimeter shooting and defensive length come April.

daily_take.headline: Arizona's March odds spike 220bps. Sharp money or March Madness hype trap?
daily_take.deck: Arizona just jumped 2.2 points to 20.9% in 24 hours on $804K volume, suggesting real money got busy. The Wildcats have talent and tourney pedigree, but 20.9% prices them as a legit Final Four contender when they're realistically a Sweet 16 team. The move feels like casual March money flooding in ahead of the tourney. Sharps should fade this. Short Arizona.
